title: quercus × hispanica
intro: Quercus × hispanica, also known as the Spanish oak or Spanish white oak, is a hybrid of the sessile oak (Quercus pellita) and the downy oak (Quercus pubescens). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Quercus × hispanica include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to support immune function and protect against oxidative stress. Quercus × hispanica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
